items.title: He made RM8,000 from recycling — and paid for surgery
items.summary: NILAI: A 74-year-old retiree has turned his recycling efforts into a source of funding for eye surgery, after collecting thousands of ringgit through a community initiative. Jaafar Idrus said he has redeemed about RM8,000 in reward points through the KitaRecycle programme under SWM Environment since taking it up in 2021. The former Malaysian Agricultural Research and Development Institute (MARDI) employee said he began recycling to stay active in retirement, but the effort later proved useful when he developed cataracts in his left eye about two years ago.

items.title: Penang water supply remains stable despite heatwave alert
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang's water supply remains robust despite the ongoing heatwave, with river and dam levels comfortably above critical thresholds, according to the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP). PBAPP chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said there was no immediate cause for concern as current raw water levels, particularly at Sungai Muda, remained stable. "Air Itam Dam is at 73.4 per cent, Teluk Bahang Dam at 86.0 per cent, and Mengkuang Dam at 91.4 per cent.

items.title: Disturbing footage of indecent act in Penang cemetery sparks police probe, two now in custody
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 24 — Police have arrested two individuals suspected of engaging in an indecent act at a cemetery in Batu Gantung, Air Itam, after a video of the incident went viral on social media. Timur Laut police chief ACP Abdul Rozak Muhammad said a report was received from a 69-year-old man about the video, which was recorded at a Chinese cemetery. “The first suspect, a 37-year-old woman, was arrested yesterday around 8.35pm at a flat near Jalan Padang Tembak.

items.title: Israel's David's Sling system failed to intercept Iranian missiles
items.summary: TEL AVIV: A malfunction in the David's Sling aerial interceptor system allowed two Iranian ballistic missiles to strike southern Israel, wounding dozens of people over the weekend, the military confirmed Monday. Israeli financial daily and website Calcalist first reported that it was David's Sling, a key component of Israel's multi-layered air defence shield, that had failed to intercept the incoming Iranian ballistic missiles. Operational since April 2017, the aerial defence system was originally designed to counter heavy rockets and cruise missiles.

items.title: Kim Teck Cheong appoints former chief secretary Mohd Zuki Ali as chairman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kim Teck Cheong Consolidated Bhd (KTC) has named Tan Sri Mohd Zuki Ali, former government chief secretary, as chairman of the Board, a move aimed at strengthening governance and supporting the company's long-term growth strategy. In a statement, KTC said that Mohd Zuki brings decades of public service and leadership experience, along with deep institutional insight. Datuk Dexter Lau, KTC executive director, said: "We are honoured to welcome Tan Sri Zuki to KTC.

items.title: Kelington bags RM413.7mil semiconductor contract in India
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kelington Group Bhd's unit, Kelington Engineering (S) Pte Ltd, has bagged a US$105 million (about RM413.7 million) contract to deliver a turnkey gas distribution system for a semiconductor wafer fabrication facility in Gujarat, India. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the group said the contract was awarded by a leading semiconductor manufacturer in India and involves the design, supply and installation of a mission-critical gas distribution system. Kelington said such systems are essential to ensure a continuous and highly controlled supply of ultra-high purity gases, supporting precision, yield and operational reliability in semiconductor fabrication processes.

items.title: Elderly mum and son found dead in Sg Petani home after more than two days
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, March 24 — An elderly woman and her son were found dead in their home in Kampung Zainol Abidin, Bedong, yesterday evening. “A report was received at 6.05pm yesterday. Initial inspection found no injuries caused by sharp weapons.

items.title: Pakistan ranked most polluted country in 2025, data shows
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Pakistan was ranked the world's smoggiest country in 2025, with concentrations of hazardous small particles known as PM2.5 up to 13 times higher than the recommended World Health Organisation level, research showed on Tuesday. Swiss air quality monitoring firm IQAir said in its annual report that 13 countries and territories kept average PM2.5 levels at the WHO standard of less than 5 microgrammes per cubic metre last year, up from seven in 2024. * Chad, statistically the smoggiest country of 2024, ranked fourth in 2025, but the decline in PM2.5 concentrations last year is likely to be the result of data gaps.

items.title: Loose cattle spark spate of road accidents in Gua Musang
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: Free-roaming livestock on several main roads in this district have been described as a "death trap" for motorists, particularly during the festive travel period. Gua Musang district police chief Superintendent Sik Choon Foo said the incidents occurred over six days as travellers returned to their hometowns for Aidilfitri. Such incidents could also result in fatal accidents, particularly for motorcyclists," he said when contacted.

items.title: 25-year-old caught driving uninsured brother's car without licence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 25-year-old man driving his brother's uninsured car without a licence was among those detained during a Road Transport Department (RTD) operation on the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway yesterday. Kuala Lumpur RTD director Hamidi Adam said the driver claimed he had borrowed the vehicle from his brother to return to his hometown for the Hari Raya holidays. Hamidi said the vehicle was seized under the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Immigration defends Hari Raya enforcement after ex-minister's criticism
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department has defended its enforcement actions during the Aidilfitri period following criticism from a former law minister over the timing of operations against undocumented migrants. Immigration deputy director-general (operations) Datuk Lokman Effendi Ramli said enforcement was carried out to maintain public order and was neither seasonal nor selective. Lokman said this approach reflects the department's commitment to consistent enforcement.

items.title: Man seen slapping shopkeeper in viral video
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A video showing a man slapping a shopkeeper has gone viral on social media, although the exact location of the incident has not been established. The video shows the man arguing with the shopkeeper before slapping him as other customers inside the grocery shop look on. The incident is believed to have started inside the shop and continued outside, where the man was restrained by a group of people.

items.title: Cops nab fake 'immigration' officer who robbed foreigners
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a 32-year-old man who allegedly posed as an immigration officer to rob foreigners. Dang Wangi police chief Assistant Commissioner Sazalee Adam said the suspect was detained at 1.30pm yesterday following a tip-off. Sazalee said investigations revealed the suspect would approach foreign men and identify himself as an immigration officer before demanding their passports.

items.title: Trump approved Iran operation after Netanyahu argued for joint killing of Khamenei
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Less than 48 hours before the US-Israeli strike on Iran began, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu spoke by phone to President Donald Trump about the reasons for launching the kind of complex, far-off war the American leader once had campaigned against. Netanyahu, determined to move forward with an operation he had urged for decades, argued that there might never be a better chance to kill Khamenei and to avenge previous Iranian efforts to assassinate Trump, these people said. Those included a murder-for-hire plot allegedly orchestrated by Iran in 2024, when Trump was a candidate.

items.title: Man beaten after allegedly trying to steal motorcycle in Ipoh
items.summary: The incident was captured in a video showing the man being surrounded, forced to the ground and assaulted by several individuals. Several bystanders and motorcycles could also be seen. The video was shared on X by user @update11111, who claimed the incident involved a group of motorcyclists.

items.title: Measat expands in South Asia with new Nepali-language channel
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Measat Global Bhd has strengthened its footprint in South Asia with the launch of a new Nepali-language channel broadcast via its Measat-3d satellite. The company is providing satellite broadcasting services for Angel TV Namaskar, which is part of the Angel TV Networks group, aimed primarily at Nepali audiences. Measat chief commercial officer Ganendra Selvaraj said the collaboration underscores the company's position as a leading satellite solutions provider in South Asia.

items.title: Airlines cancel more flights as Middle East conflict escalates
items.summary: Flights to Dubai have been cancelled until April 19 and to Riyadh until April 18. AIRBALTIC Latvia's airBaltic said all flights to Tel Aviv had been cancelled until April 29. All flights to Dubai stand cancelled until October 24.

items.title: Steven Sim's aide lodges police report over false claim on hospital relocation
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: An aide to Bukit Mertajam member of parliament Steven Sim Chee Keong has lodged a police report over "defamatory and provocative" social media claims regarding the alleged relocation of Bukit Mertajam Hospital. Lim Zheng Han said the report was made today following a Facebook post containing elements of racial and religious incitement alongside inaccurate claims about the facility. "However, the post was accompanied by an unfounded caption claiming that 'Bukit Mertajam Hospital will be relocated to avoid disturbing an Indian temple — Steven Sim Chee Keong'," he said today.

items.title: Couple remanded over viral indecent act at Penang cemetery
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police have obtained a three-day remand order against a couple allegedly caught on video engaging in an indecent act at a cemetery here. Earlier, the couple arrived in handcuffs under police escort. This morning, state police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail confirmed that police have detained the couple.

items.title: Vietnam Airlines plans domestic route cuts due to jet fuel shortage, regulator says
items.summary: HANOI: Flag carrier Vietnam Airlines plans to cancel 23 flights per week across several domestic routes from April because of looming jet fuel shortages, Vietnam's aviation authority said. Vietnam Airlines, part of state-owned Vietnam Airlines Corp, is prioritising routes critical to national connectivity, trade, tourism, diplomacy and domestic travel, the CAAV said in a statement issued late on Monday. Vietnamese airlines are preparing to implement fuel surcharges on international routes from early April, the authority added.

items.title: Solarvest's LSS 5+ win signals growth momentum
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Solarvest Holdings Bhd's first successful bid under the large-scale solar (LSS) 5+ programme in March underscores the company's continued execution momentum, despite a recent pullback in its share price. According to RHB Research, the project also represents a steady step towards its RM2 billion order book target by financial year 2026 (FY26), according to RHB Research. The contract, secured via its subsidiary Atlantic Blue Sdn Bhd, involves an engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) agreement with Jubli Pasifik Sdn Bhd to develop a 36 megawatt alternating current (MWac) solar photovoltaic plant in Padang Siding, Perlis.

items.title: Yinson set for stable FPSO income, backed by US$19.5bil backlog
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Yinson Holdings Bhd is expected to maintain stable earnings from its floating production, storage and offloading (FPSO) charter operations, supported by a US$19.5 billion backlog that underpins its long-term earnings, RHB Research said. "We remain constructive on Yinson's medium-term outlook, underpinned by a growing base of recurring FPSO charter income and a strong US$19.5 billion backlog providing long-term earnings visibility," he said in a note. Yinson posted FY26 revenue of RM5.4 billion, down 28 per cent year-on-year, primarily due to lower engineering, procurement, construction, installation and commissioning contributions following the start of charter periods for FPSO Maria Quitéria, FPSO Agogo and FPSO Atlanta.

items.title: 'No one above the law': Dzulkefly on ministry staff smoking at restaurant
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Reaffirming his stance against double standards, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad stressed that no one is above the law following a smoking violation by a ministry official at an eatery. The officer was issued a compound notice for smoking at a gazetted premises where the act is strictly prohibited. The statement follows a report that the Health Ministry issued a compound notice to the officer for violating the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act (Act 852).

items.title: Japan to release national oil reserves from Thursday
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan will release another part of its strategic oil reserves from Thursday, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said Tuesday, after Tokyo began to tap stockpiles last week. Last Monday, Tokyo started the release of 15 days' worth of private-sector petroleum reserves. Japan depends on the Middle East for around 95 percent of its oil imports.

items.title: [UPDATED] Gerakan acts as moderating force in PN, says Lau
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Gerakan can serve as a moderating force within Perikatan Nasional (PN) to address issues affecting non-Muslims, its president Datuk Dr Dominic Lau said. Lau said Gerakan, as a multi-ethnic party, has the potential to gain broad appeal among Malaysians. Lau said the party election has been postponed to ensure unity ahead of the looming general election.

items.title: Yinson shares up despite weaker FY26 net profit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Yinson Holdings Bhd shares were up in early trading despite the company's net profit declining to RM683 million for the financial year ended Jan 31, 2026 (FY26) from RM1.24 billion a year ago. At 10.44am, Yinson stock rose four sen to RM2.33 with 2.26 million shares traded. In a research note today, Kenanga Investment Bank Bhd said Yinson's FY26 results came slightly above expectations due to lower-than-expected green energy losses.

items.title: Explainer: Why flights are getting costlier
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Jet fuel remains one of the most significant cost components for airlines, typically accounting for 20 to 25 per cent of total operating expenses. Prior to the recent geopolitical escalation in the Middle East, prices were relatively stable at around US$85 to US$90 per barrel. However, the sharp surge to between US$150 and US$200 per barrel has dramatically changed the cost landscape, placing intense strain on airline finances globally.

items.title: Smooth traffic on major highways, surge expected from noon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic on main highways heading towards the Klang Valley remains smooth and under control this morning. A Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) spokesperson said traffic is expected to increase from noon until late evening as travellers return home following the Aidilfitri holidays. PLUS has activated the Smart Lane at 34 locations from 9am to 10pm today to ensure smoother traffic flow, he said.

items.title: Two killed in car-motorcycle collision
items.summary: IPOH: Two men were killed in an accident involving a car and a motorcycle at Kampung Seri Makmur, Batu 7, near Batu Kurau early this morning. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received an emergency call at 1.24am. A team from the Batu Kurau station was immediately dispatched to the scene.

items.title: Over 50 urns stolen in a month: What we know so far
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: More than 50 burial urns have been stolen from three columbaria across three memorial parks in a month, prompting a federal investigation into the thefts. The thefts first came to light on March 9, when more than 20 urns were reported missing from the Perpetual Memorial Park in Kulai, Johor. Xiao En said it was conducting an internal investigation while assisting police.

items.title: Bahrain, a kingdom deeply proud of its seafaring heritage
items.summary: MANAMA (Bahrain): "Did you see the bubbles?" Abbas Al Faraj, a licensed tour guide in Bahrain, pointed towards the shallow waters near Bahrain Fort (Qal'at al-Bahrain) on the northern coast of the island, where steady bursts of water rise quietly from beneath the seabed. "That is Bahrain," he said.

items.title: Johor petrol station under probe after viral videos of subsidised RON95 poured into car boot container
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 24 — The Johor Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) is investigating a petrol station in Mutiara Rini here that allegedly allowed two people to fill a container with subsidised RON95 petrol instead of directly into their vehicle tank. Its director Lilis Saslinda Pornomo said investigators will be looking into the petrol station’s closed-circuit television recordings, as well as examining transaction records and purchase receipts for verification purposes. “We found two transactions involving RON95 petrol purchases, with one into the vehicle’s tank, while the other was into a plastic container in the vehicle’s boot with a quantity that exceeded the limit allowed by the Malaysian Supply Controller.

items.title: MARKET PULSE AM MAR 24, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia opened higher on Tuesday, tracking overnight gains on Wall Street after US President Donald Trump extended the deadline for possible strikes on Iranian infrastructure. Crude oil prices also declined, with Brent crude falling below US$100 per barrel. In the crypto market, Bitcoin rose to RM278,967.

items.title: Your shopping bill is about to get more expensive: Farmers say veggie prices could rise 50pc in April
items.summary: Cameron Highlands Malay Farmers Association chairman Datuk Syed Abd Rahman Syed Abd Rashid said the hike could happen within the next week or two, New Strait Times reported yesterday. Malaysia Muslim Consumers Association chief activist Nadzim Johan advised consumers to spend wisely amid the looming price hikes. “Consumers have the power to influence prices.

items.title: Education Ministry expands School in Hospital programme to Penang, helping students stay on track
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) is continuously striving to meet the needs of every segment of students, including those undergoing treatment in hospitals, says Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek. Fadhlina said the continuous efforts were also reflected in the Malaysia Education Plan (RPM) 2026-2035, which emphasised inclusive education and equitable access. The SDH programme has been implemented since 2011.

items.title: Pop-up fireworks stall in JB goes up in flames, damages nearby Proton Gen-2 and food truck
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 24 — A stall selling firecrackers and fireworks was destroyed in a fire here last night. Larkin Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) operations commander, Senior Fire Officer I Nakish Nekmat said 11 personnel were rushed to the fire at Majidee Corner 1C, Jalan Bunga Ros, Kampung Dato Sulaiman Menteri, after receiving an emergency call at 8.32 pm. “The pop-up tent used for selling the firecrackers and fireworks was 90 per cent destroyed,” he said in a statement, adding that the fire was brought under control at 8.42 pm, and mop-up operations are currently ongoing.

items.title: Mum and two children dead, two others hurt in Perodua Aruz skid crash near Dungun
items.summary: DUNGUN, March 24 — A mother and her two children were killed after the sport utility vehicle (SUV) they were travelling in skidded at Kilometre 59 of Jalan Jerangau-Jabor, near Jerangau Felda here, yesterday. In the 6.10 pm incident, two of them died at the scene, while the other died at the Jerangau Health Clinic. Dungun police chief Supt Maizura Abdul Kadir, who confirmed the incident, said further details will follow.

items.title: Abuse Budi95, lose it: Finance Ministry to freeze cards over subsidised fuel misuse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — The Ministry of Finance (MOF) will block the cards of individuals found to have abused the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) assistance programme by filling subsidised RON95 petrol into empty barrels or containers, as has gone viral recently. MOF stressed that such action clearly violates regulations because it constitutes misuse of fuel subsidies under the Budi95 initiative and could affect the government’s efforts to ensure transparent, targeted and effective subsidy management. The government, the MOF said, will not compromise on any form of subsidy leakages and misuse, especially amid current uncertainties that require close control over fuel supply and prices.

items.title: JPJ suspends two officers over viral offences: one a double-line breach, another a fatal crash
items.summary: IPOH, March 24 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has suspended two of its personnel to facilitate investigations into two separate incidents involving the department’s vehicle, namely a viral video showing a vehicle crossing double lines, and a fatal crash case. He said the two incidents involved different situations, one related to a viral video showing a JPJ vehicle crossing double lines and the other involving a crash. “The first incident involves a viral video of a JPJ vehicle crossing double lines along the East-West Highway (JRTB), which falls under the department’s internal investigation.

items.title: A slap on the wrist: Existing legal gaps weaken crackdown on syndicated cable theft, among key challenges, says Prasarana
items.summary: “Law enforcement like the Royal Malaysia Police has been very helpful in curbing cable theft but their hands have been tied for a long time. Idzqandar said lenient legal deterrence has resulted in rising cases of cable thefts and repeated offenders. — Picture by Yusof Mat Isa Idzqandar said law enforcement authorities typically investigate cable theft incidents under Section 379 of the Penal Code for theft, but suspects are often ultimately prosecuted under Sections 447 for criminal trespass instead.

items.title: MOH confirms civil servants caught smoking at Rawang eatery, compounds imposed
items.summary: The MOH said compounds have been imposed on the individuals under Section 16(2) (No 4: smoking at eating places) of the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852). Earlier today, a post went viral on X depicting individuals serving with the MOH smoking at a restaurant in Rawang. “The MOH greatly appreciates the public’s concerns and encourages the channelling of information or complaints through official MOH channels to strengthen enforcement to protect public health,” it added.

items.title: Sultan of Pahang urges public to stay vigilant amid hot, dry weather
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 23 — The Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah has urged the public to take extra precautions amid the current hot and dry weather affecting the state. Al-Sultan Abdullah also urged the public to refrain from open burning, including the disposal of rubbish, dry leaves and garden waste, as it could trigger uncontrolled fires and worsen air quality. “Open burning is not only dangerous but can also cause haze and affect public health.

items.title: KL-Butterworth ETS delayed by over 2.5 hours after technical glitch at Rasa station
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Electric Train Service (ETS) EP9130 from KL Sentral to Butterworth is expected to experience a delay of about 157 minutes following a technical issue at Rasa station, Hulu Selangor. Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) said in a statement that due to the technical glitch, the train, which departed KL Sentral at 1.40 pm, is now expected to reach Butterworth Station at 8.22 pm instead of the original 5.45 pm. “The technical glitch at Rasa station occurred at 3.02 pm today,” said KTMB.

items.title: Former minister Yeo Bee Yin and husband announce arrival of identical twin sons
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Puchong Member of Parliament Yeo Bee Yin and her husband, Lee Yeow Seng, have announced the arrival of their identical twin boys. In her social media post, Yeo shared details about the twins, who are classified as monochorionic diamniotic (MCDA), meaning they share a single placenta but develop in separate amniotic sacs. “Yup, two more boys have joined the LeeBro team.

items.title: Elderly man dies, five injured in two-vehicle crash near Gerik-Jeli highway R&R
items.summary: IPOH, March 23 — An elderly man was killed while five others were injured in a collision involving two vehicles near the Puncak Titiwangsa Rest and Service (R&R) area on the Gerik-Jeli East-West Highway (JRTB) today. Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 11.24 am. He said a team of 17 personnel and five fire tenders from the Gerik and Jeli Fire and Rescue Stations were dispatched to the scene.

items.title: Heavy traffic reported on major highways as Malaysians return to Kuala Lumpur after Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Traffic on several major highways nationwide was heavy as of 5 pm today as city dwellers began returning to the capital after the Aidilfitri holidays. A Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) spokesperson said congestion along the North-South Expressway (PLUS) E1 affected both northbound and southbound directions due to a surge in return traffic ahead of the resumption of work tomorrow. “Traffic on the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway (KLK) was congested only in the westbound direction (towards Kuala Lumpur), while eastbound traffic remained smooth,” the spokesperson said.

items.title: Rising voters in Tawau spark plan to to create second parliamentary seat
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 23 — Tawau could be divided into two parliamentary constituencies following a sharp increase in the number of registered voters in the area, according to Datuk Nizam Abu Bakar Titingan. Sabah Youth, Sports and Creative Economy Development Minister said the current Tawau parliamentary seat has more than 80,000 voters, highlighting the need for better representation, The Star reported today. The Tawau federal seat also covers three state constituencies: Apas, Balung and Sri Tanjong.

items.title: As temperatures soar, Kedah and Perlis worry about drying rivers and shrinking reservoirs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Residents in northern peninsular Malaysia are facing severe water shortages and rising temperatures as a prolonged heatwave continues to grip Kedah and Perlis. In Kampung Lubuk Tualang, Sik, Sungai Tebal is running unusually shallow, exposing sandbanks in several areas, while local hill water sources have dried up, forcing villagers to ration their limited supplies, Harian Metro reported today. The extreme heat is also affecting agriculture, with rice fields outside the Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) areas reporting poor yields due to limited irrigation.

items.title: Kedah police checking hospitals after viral claims of ‘mercun bola’ injuries in Pendang
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 23 — Police are obtaining further information from the hospital regarding claims that several individuals were injured after being hit by a ‘mercun bola’ (ball firecracker) in Tobiar, Pendang. Police will visit the hospital today to obtain full details regarding the incident. Earlier, the incident went viral on Facebook, showing pictures of several injured individuals with the caption: ‘tragedi pakar bom dari Tobiar, Pendang, Kedah..ujikaji custom mercun bola, tujuh mangsa beraya di hospital’ (‘Tragedy involving bomb expert from Tobiar, Pendang, Kedah… custom ball firecracker experiment, seven victims celebrating Aidilfitri in hospital).

items.title: Bukit Mertajam Hospital dismisses claims of relocation to avoid disrupting temple
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, March 23 — Bukit Mertajam Hospital’s Board of Visitors today dismissed allegations that the hospital would be relocated to avoid disrupting a temple. Board spokesperson Ng Yi Cheah, who lodged a police report today over the false claims, said the allegations appeared on social media on March 22. “The post titled ‘Hospital Bukit Mertajam akan dipindahkan agar tak ganggu kuil - Steven Sim Chee Keong’ was false, baseless, misleading and malicious in intent,” he said in a statement after filing the report at the Seberang Perai Tengah police headquarters.

items.title: Bangladeshi man drowns after falling into river in Perak
items.summary: IPOH, March 23 — A Bangladeshi man was found drowned after falling into the river near Kledang Timur 4, Taman Bandar Baru Menglembu, here, earlier this morning. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the 29-year-old’s body was recovered after the department received an emergency call from the public at 8.35 am. He said 10 personnel and two fire engines from the Pasir Puteh Fire and Rescue Station were deployed to the scene.

items.title: Johor Immigration cites extensive borders, evolving syndicate tactics as key challenges
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 23 — Johor’s geographical challenges, with its extensive land and maritime borders, as well as the increasingly deceptive tactics used by illegal immigrant syndicates, which frequently change their modus operandi, are among the main challenges faced by the state Immigration Department in curbing illegal activities. State Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said these factors have led to the existence of numerous ‘rat trails’ (illegal routes) along the coastline, with areas such as Kota Tinggi, Mersing and Pontian becoming primary targets for syndicates smuggling illegal immigrants from Indonesia. Declining to reveal the exact hotspot locations, he said that the syndicates take advantage of illegal sea routes, using small boats at night to bring in illegal immigrants, especially from Indonesia.

items.title: Health Ministry: Malaysia’s medicine supply stable despite West Asia conflict
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 23 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has confirmed that the supply of medicines in the country is currently stable and under control despite the conflict in West Asia. In a statement today, MOH said that the short-term impact of the West Asia conflict is minimal based on current assessments. “The stock level of medicines at MOH facilities is maintained at between one and three months based on usage requirements, and is supported by additional buffer stock of up to two months at the concession company level for medicines supplied through the logistics concession,” it said.

items.title: After 40 years in the dark, Marudi village lights up just in time for Raya
items.summary: Deputy Minister in the Sarawak Premier’s Department (Labour, Immigration and Project Monitoring) Datuk Gerawat Gala said the electricity connection to the village was completed two weeks ago, just in time for the Raya celebration. “Kampung Seberang Lubok Nibong holds a special place in my heart as it is the only Muslim village in DUN N.78 Mulu,” he said, adding that the village has about 60 residents. Also present was the village headman Madlie Moss.

items.title: Future of Kapit seat: Nanta signals Alvin Runggah as next parliamentary candidate
items.summary: KAPIT, March 23 — Kapit MP Dato Sri Alexander Nanta Linggi has signalled that Datuk Alvin Runggah Jimbun is the person he intends to entrust with the Kapit parliamentary seat if he no longer contests in future elections. “If the time comes when I no longer contest as a parliamentary candidate for P215 Kapit, Datuk Alvin Runggah Jimbun will be the individual who will take over that responsibility. “Therefore, he needs the full support of the Kapit community to ensure that development continuity can be carried forward successfully,” he said during a Thanksgiving Dinner here on Sunday.

items.title: From 82 to 99? Sarawak assembly approves 17 new state seats, but Election Commission has final say
items.summary: KUCHING, March 23 — The Sarawak Legislative Assembly (DUN) has approved an increase in the number of state seats, expanding from 82 to 99 following the addition of 17 new constituencies. “At this stage, there is no report yet from SPR on whether the 17 new constituencies have been confirmed. However, increasing parliamentary seats would require a two-thirds majority in Parliament, which may be more challenging for the ruling government.

items.title: Soldier killed in crash involving two motorcycles and car in Alor Gajah
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, March 23 — A soldier was killed in a crash involving two motorcycles and a car along Jalan Padang Kemunting, Masjid Tanah here, early this morning. Melaka Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) chief Supt Muhammad Zaki Ramat said the crash also involved a SYM VF3i motorcycle and a Proton Saga car. “He then collided with a motorcycle and a car travelling in the opposite direction, from Pengkalan Balak towards Sungai Udang.

items.title: NRES strengthens weather monitoring with AI, big data to tackle climate change
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 23 — The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES), through the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), is strengthening weather monitoring and forecasting capabilities to ensure the country remains prepared for climate change. Its minister, Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup, said the effort includes integrating artificial intelligence (AI) and big data analytics into the Multi-Hazard Early Warning System (MHEWS), in line with current climate challenges. Arthur said strengthening the National Climate Centre of Excellence is also a key focus to support research on climate change impacts and guide the development of more sustainable and resilient national adaptation policies.

items.title: Penang to launch first direct flight from Qingdao, boosting tourism and trade links
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 23 — Penang is set to achieve a significant milestone with the launch of its first direct flight connecting Qingdao, China and the state on March 31, marking a major step in enhancing air connectivity between Malaysia and China. State Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said that the new route, to be operated by Qingdao Airlines, represents a crucial development in strengthening bilateral ties, particularly in tourism, trade and cultural exchanges “With the introduction of this route, Qingdao will become the northernmost city in China currently connected to Penang via direct flights. “This important breakthrough reflects the steady northward expansion of Penang’s air connectivity with China, progressively enhancing our network coverage and strengthening overall connectivity,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Safety first, celebration second: Housing minister orders review after seventh fireworks stall fire destroys property
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming has ordered a review of standard operating procedures (SOPs) for temporary fireworks stalls following a fire in Nibong Tebal, Penang yesterday. Both the Fire and Rescue Department and the Local Government Department were directed to conduct the review, The New Straits Times reported today. According to The New Straits Times , the Taman Pekaka fire is the seventh fire involving fireworks stalls reported in Malaysia this year.
